The family Pholcidae, also known as cellar spiders or daddy long legs, is a group of arachnids characterized by their long, thin legs and irregular web-building habits. Metagonia myrtifolia is one of the many species within this family, with its specific classification providing insight into its evolutionary relationships and behavioral traits.
